message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Product criteria reminders for Tasks 9 and 20">
Good to hear Task 7 is solid with 28 integration tests covering the criteria. 

For the active tasks -- quick reminders on the product criteria:

**Task 9 (Delete, US-7):**
- Delete button has accessible label including the todo title (e.g., "Delete: Buy groceries")
- Lightweight confirmation before deletion (your `details/summary` proposal is great for this)
- After deletion, focus moves to next item (or add input if list is now empty)
- Transitions to empty state when last item deleted
- `aria-live` announces "Todo deleted"
- Authorization: cannot delete another user's todo

**Task 20 (Edit, US-8):**
- Pencil icon button as primary affordance (not click-on-title)
- Edit mode: input pre-filled with current title, visible label for screen readers
- Enter to save, Escape to cancel (or explicit Save/Cancel buttons)
- Same validation as add (non-empty, max 300 chars)
- Focus management: entering edit -> focus input; saving/canceling -> focus returns to item
- No-JS baseline: navigate to edit page, save redirects back

Let me know if any product questions come up on either task.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good reminders. Let me check if the edit (Task 20) CSS needs to be prepared, since that task is also marked active.
No edit CSS exists yet. Let me add it proactively since Task 20 is active. The edit pattern needs: an edit button (pencil icon), an edit mode with input field, and save/cancel buttons.

content.content: The file /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css has been updated successfully.

lines: +   * Pencil icon button triggers edit mode (US-8). No-JS baseline
+   * navigates to an edit page; HTMX swaps inline. */
+  /* Edit form replaces the title when in edit mode */

input.command:
git add /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add edit mode CSS for inline todo title editing (Task 20)

Styles for pencil edit button, inline edit form with input,
and save/cancel buttons per US-8. Edit button muted by default,
primary on hover. Save button uses primary color, cancel is
text-styled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push
input.description: Commit and push edit mode CSS

input.content:
Thanks for the criteria reminders. CSS is now ready for both active tasks:

**Task 9 (Delete)**: Committed in `5bdf62e` -- `details/summary` confirmation pattern with `.todo-item__delete-confirm`, delete trigger, confirm form, and danger-colored "Yes" button.

**Task 20 (Edit)**: Just committed in `0e3c000` -- `.todo-item__edit` pencil button (muted, primary on hover), `.todo-item__edit-form` inline form with input, `.todo-item__edit-save` (primary) and `.todo-item__edit-cancel` (text-styled) buttons.

Both pushed. The Rust teammates can use these class names when building the templates. The focus management (moving focus to input on edit, returning on save/cancel) will be handled by the HTMX swap or JS enhancement layer -- the CSS just provides the visual foundation.
input.summary: CSS ready for both Task 9 and Task 20, pushed

routing.summary: CSS ready for both Task 9 and Task 20, pushed
routing.content:
Thanks for the criteria reminders. CSS is now ready for both active tasks:

**Task 9 (Delete)**: Committed in `5bdf62e` -- `details/summary` confirmation pattern with `.todo-item__delete-confirm`, delete trigger, confirm form, and danger-colored "Yes" button.

**Task 20 (Edit)**: Just committed in `0e3c000` -- `.todo-item__edit` pencil button (muted, primary on hover), `.todo-item__edit-form` inline form with input, `.todo-item__edit-save` (primary) and `.todo-item__edit-cancel` (text-styled) buttons.

Both pushed. The Rust teammates can use these class names when building the templates. The focus management (moving focus to input on edit, returning on save/cancel) will be handled by the HTMX swap or JS enhancement layer -- the CSS just provides the visual foundation.

content.text: All CSS for Tasks 9 and 20 is committed and pushed. Standing by for any feedback or adjustments as the templates get built.
